skill.name: internal-comms
skill.description: A set of resources to help me write all kinds of internal communications, using the formats that my company likes to use. Claude should use this skill whenever asked to write some sort of internal communications (status reports, leadership updates, 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, project updates, etc.).
skill.summary: Helps teams draft consistent 3P updates, newsletters, FAQs, and other internal communications.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 11 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections match normal writing guidance, and the backtick detections are markdown file paths, not command execution. No prompt injection, executable code, data exfiltration, or unauthorized network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positives
low_findings.description: Static detections at SKILL.md line 3, SKILL.md line 29, examples/company-newsletter.md line 4, and examples/general-comms.md line 8 are ordinary communication text, not cryptographic code or configuration. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose about communication formats and clarification questions. There is no hash function, cipher selection, key handling, or executable cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: System reconnaissance false positives
low_findings.description: Static detections at examples/company-newsletter.md line 7 are writing-style guidance to use first-person company voice. They do not inspect host systems, enumerate files, or gather machine information. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line only discusses wording such as using the company's collective voice. It has no system commands, environment probes, or reconnaissance instructions.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: Markdown backticks are not shell execution
low_findings.description: Static detections at SKILL.md lines 22 through 26 are markdown inline code spans naming files in the examples directory. They are not Ruby backtick operators or shell command execution. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap static documentation paths such as examples/3p-updates.md. No interpreter, command string, subprocess call, or user-controlled execution path is present.

content.value_statement: Internal updates often become inconsistent, too detailed, or hard to scan. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical formats for newsletters, 3P updates, FAQs, and general company messages.
actual_capabilities: Formats Progress, Plans, Problems updates for teams or larger organizations
Drafts company-wide newsletter updates for Slack and email
Creates FAQ-style answers for common employee questions
Guides general internal messages by audience, purpose, tone, and format
Suggests source types to review, including Slack, email, calendar, and documents
Keeps updates concise and focused on company-wide impact
limitations: Requires user-provided context or authorized access to company sources
Does not verify facts beyond the information available in the conversation or connected tools
Does not replace legal, HR, security, or executive review for sensitive announcements
Does not send messages or publish content by itself

use_cases.title: Prepare weekly 3P updates
use_cases.description: Convert team progress, plans, and blockers into a short executive-ready update.

use_cases.title: Write company newsletters
use_cases.description: Summarize major announcements, leadership updates, milestones, and relevant links for broad distribution.

use_cases.title: Answer repeated employee questions
use_cases.description: Turn recurring questions into clear FAQ entries with concise answers and links to authoritative sources.
use_cases.target_user: People operations and leadership support teams

prompt_templates.title: Draft a simple 3P update
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a team update
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a 3P update for the [team name] team covering [date range]. Progress: [facts]. Plans: [facts]. Problems: [facts]. Keep it concise and data-driven.

prompt_templates.title: Create a company newsletter
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for broad company communication
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a company-wide newsletter for [week or month]. Use these announcements, documents, and links: [source list]. Group related updates into sections and make it suitable for Slack and email.

prompt_templates.title: Build an employee FAQ
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for repeated employee questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these employee questions and source notes: [questions and sources]. Create FAQ entries with one-sentence questions and concise answers. Flag any answer that needs official review.

prompt_templates.title: Shape a sensitive leadership message
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an internal communication about [topic] for [audience]. Purpose: [purpose]. Tone: [tone]. Required facts: [facts]. Risks or sensitivities: [risks]. Keep important information first and mark claims that need approval.

output_examples.input: Draft a 3P update for the platform team using notes about latency improvements, next week's migration, and staffing blockers.
output_examples.output: A concise 3P update with an emoji header, a Progress section for latency work, a Plans section for migration tasks, and a Problems section for staffing constraints.

output_examples.input: Create a company newsletter from leadership announcements, product milestones, hiring updates, and links to source documents.
output_examples.output: A Slack and email-ready newsletter grouped into company announcements, progress on priorities, leadership updates, and social updates.

output_examples.input: Turn repeated employee questions about a new planning process into an FAQ.
output_examples.output: A set of short question-and-answer entries that cite official sources and flag unresolved questions for leadership review.

best_practices: Use official source material before drafting broad company messages
Confirm the audience, purpose, tone, and required format before writing
Send sensitive or high-impact communications through the correct approval process
anti_patterns: Do not invent facts, metrics, links, or executive positions
Do not include private employee details unless they are approved for the audience
Do not use the newsletter format for narrow team-only updates

faq.question: What communication formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports 3P updates, company newsletters, FAQ responses, status reports, leadership updates, project updates, incident reports, and general internal messages.

faq.question: Does the skill need access to Slack, email, calendar, or documents?
faq.answer: No. It can work from user-provided notes, but authorized sources can improve accuracy and coverage.

faq.question: Can it publish or send messages automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill helps draft and structure communication. The user remains responsible for review and distribution.

faq.question: How should sensitive announcements be handled?
faq.answer: Sensitive topics should be reviewed by the appropriate legal, HR, security, or executive stakeholders before sharing.

faq.question: Can it adapt tone for different audiences?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for audience, purpose, tone, and format when the request does not match a specific template.

faq.question: Does it verify whether the information is true?
faq.answer: It can use available sources and flag uncertainty, but it does not independently verify facts beyond the provided context.
